lines.original: Show Mxrci on it! he-he-he!
lines.translated: Show Merc on it! he-he-he!
lines.note: “Mxrci” is a stylized spelling referring to a Merc/Mercedes (brand).

lines.original: (Haan)
lines.translated: (Yes)

lines.original: Do do ghode Magna ni
lines.translated: I don't ask for two horses

lines.original: Char G-Wagon'an ni
lines.translated: I don't ask for four G‑Wagons

lines.original: Utte nu main chhe chhe inch chakkiyan
lines.translated: I put six-by-six-inch rims on them
lines.note: “Chakkiyan” here refers to wheels/rims (vehicle customization).

lines.original: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.translated: It takes time to finish them

lines.original: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.translated: to bring us down

lines.original: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an
lines.translated: Just once, step on it up to sixty
lines.note: “Chhatti'an” = sixty (likely referring to speed); literal: press the gas to sixty.

lines.original: Fan ikko naar da main
lines.translated: I'm a fan of the same woman
lines.note: The word 'fan' is likely English 'fan' (admirer); 'naar' here is taken as 'woman'—the original is slightly ambiguous.

lines.original: Das vaari vaarda main
lines.translated: I strike ten times

lines.original: Ohde utton sau-sau diyan gatthiyan
lines.translated: Hundreds and hundreds of bundles on them

lines.original: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.translated: It'll take time to be wiped away

lines.original: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.translated: To bring us down

lines.original: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti
lines.translated: Hit the chest hard once

lines.original: Vall kaddta rekaane hunn tutte na crease
lines.translated: I pulled out the edges; now they won't tear or crease
lines.note: Some words ('vall', 'rekaane') are unclear/ambiguous in the original; this is a conservative literal reading (edges/margins pulled out, so no tearing/creasing).

lines.original: Thoda rukhe aa subaah de sanu aaundi ni tameez
lines.translated: We're a bit rough in the mornings; we don't know manners

lines.original: Aa lai tainu ditti saambh laa ni bebe wali chunni
lines.translated: Take it — I gave it to you; keep that girl's scarf safe.
lines.note: 'bebe' (beeba/beeba) = girl / dear girl (see glossary).

lines.original: Baitha double R vich paake bapu di kameez
lines.translated: Sitting in a Double R, wearing my father's shirt.
lines.note: 'Double R' likely refers to a Rolls‑Royce (luxury car).

lines.original: Bahla door ni rekaane hun Ranjheyan nu kheda
lines.translated: I won't keep the Ranjhas at a distance now; I'll play with them.
lines.note: 'Ranjheyan' alludes to Ranjha (romantic figures) — here meaning lovers/rivals.

lines.original: Bahla nede nu na jawan bas jhaaka ee bada mera
lines.translated: Don't go near; just one look from me is enough.
lines.note: 'jhaaka' = a strong stare/glance (literal; implies power of a look).

lines.original: Bahla ruka na rekaane kite maaran ikk fera
lines.translated: Don't let them stay around — otherwise someone might land one strike.
lines.note: Line is idiomatic; 'fera' can mean a hit/turn — rendered conservatively as 'strike'.

lines.original: Naddi do vall kha gayi ditta ikk vaari geda
lines.translated: The stream swallowed both sides and gave one round.
lines.note: Ambiguous imagery: 'naddi' = stream/river, 'geda' = lap/round; literal conservative rendering.

lines.original: Kithe dabeya rekaane ehna dekh layi dabake
lines.translated: Where did I press them to keep, pressing down just to look at them?
lines.note: Contains colloquial 'dabake' (pressing); phrasing is idiomatic and ambiguous, rendered literally.

lines.original: Karaan shopping'an Milan Afghan 'ali kha ke
lines.translated: I go shopping — Milan, Afghan 'Ali and the like.
lines.note: 'Milan' likely the city/brand; 'Afghan "Ali"' is unclear (possibly a brand/place/name) — kept literal.

lines.original: Tera darji rekaane change karde Mohali
lines.translated: We have your tailor make changes in Mohali.
lines.note: 'darji' means 'tailor'. 'rekaane' is a Punjabi verb here meaning to arrange or set up.

lines.original: Jutti pairan wich paavan tere Paris'on manga ke
lines.translated: I put shoes on my feet that I ordered for you from Paris.

lines.original: Tin mahine tin change ne location'an rekaane
lines.translated: Every three months we change locations.

lines.original: Nitt UAE ton UK nu rotation'an rekaane
lines.translated: We keep rotating from the UAE to the UK.

lines.original: Main taan rukja dimag sala rukda ni mera
lines.translated: I don't stop — damn, my mind never stops.
lines.note: 'sala' is an expletive/filler commonly used in Punjabi; rendered here as 'damn'.

lines.original: Tan hi laa laiye samadhi meditation'an rekaane
lines.translated: So we even arrange samadhi meditations.
lines.note: 'samadhi' refers to deep or intense meditation (Sanskrit/Punjabi usage).

lines.original: Loki sade kolon sikh hunn sanu den mattan
lines.translated: People now learn from us; they don't give to us.
lines.note: Literal reading: 'den mattan' = 'they don't give (to) us.' This line is a bit ambiguous in intent but translated conservatively.

lines.original: Sanu kinna kuj de giyan ni mitti diyan vattan
lines.translated: How much have they given us? Nothing — just earthen pots.
lines.note: 'mitti diyan vattan' literally means 'earthen pots'; the line implies they gave very little.

lines.original: Jithon langhe tera yaar maare camera flash
lines.translated: Wherever your friend walks by, he flashes the camera.

lines.original: Hun khinch'de ne photo kade khinch de si lattan
lines.translated: Now they take photos; back then they used to pull lattan.
lines.note: 'lattan' is regional/slang and ambiguous here; I left it literal.

lines.original: Kehndi akh na lagge ve jad di mari tere utte
lines.translated: She says, "may the evil eye not fall on the one who's fallen for you."
lines.note: 'akh na lagge' is a protective phrase meaning 'may the evil eye not strike.'

lines.original: Sanu sidha ne banage ni halaat sade puthe
lines.translated: Our circumstances haven't straightened out for our boys.
lines.note: 'puthe' likely means 'boys' or 'guys' (informal).

lines.original: Tu taan hune ee tang aagyi biba nindran udake
lines.translated: Girl, you've already started to trouble me, stealing my sleep.
lines.note: Uses glossary term 'biba' = 'girl / dear girl'.

lines.original: Sanu kai saal hogye naiyo changi tarah sutte
lines.translated: It's been many years since we've slept properly.

lines.original: Naara thaar ke te vairi rakhan saarh ke rekaane
lines.translated: Shouting slogans and keeping enemies tucked away.
lines.note: 'Naara thaar ke' = raising slogans; 'saarh ke rekaane' is idiomatic/ambiguous, rendered conservatively as 'tucked away.'

lines.original: Peende motran te chahan pattu kaarh ke rekaane
lines.translated: Riding in cars and showing off pattu to arouse desire.
lines.note: 'motran' = motors/cars; 'pattu' is regional/slang (literal left as 'pattu' concept: showy cloth/pride), phrasing kept conservative.

lines.original: Tere bull'an de maare ne main vi ek M laata
lines.translated: Because of your bullets, I too brought an M.
lines.note: “bull'an” likely means 'bullets' or 'shots'; “M” is ambiguous (could be a model initial or weapon). Conservative literal rendering used.

lines.original: Haale navi aa Ferrari jutti jhaad ke rekaane
lines.translated: The Ferrari's still new; I park it after kicking off my shoes.

lines.original: Dil todte main chhe chhe saak modte
lines.translated: Breaking hearts, I twist them six times.
lines.note: Phrase “chhe chhe saak modte” is idiomatic/unclear; rendered conservatively as repeated twisting (six times).

lines.original: Kataran wich atharan hor pakkian
lines.translated: Eighteen more are packed in the lines.

lines.original: Duron matha tekdiyan
lines.translated: They bow their heads from afar.

lines.original: Mud mud dekh diyan
lines.translated: They keep looking back.

lines.original: Assi naiyo ikk vaari takkiyan
lines.translated: We don't look just once.
lines.note: “takkiyan” here refers to looking/staring; translated conservatively as 'look'.

lines.original: Fan ikko naar da main
lines.translated: I'm a fan of only one 'naar'.
lines.note: The word “naar” is ambiguous in this line (could mean 'fire', a woman, or figurative passion); kept literal and quoted.

lines.original: Das vaari vaarda main
lines.translated: I strike ten times

lines.original: Ohde utton sau sau diyan gatthiyan
lines.translated: Hundreds and hundreds of knots on her

lines.original: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.translated: It takes time to erase

lines.original: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.translated: To bring us down

lines.original: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an
lines.translated: Just once, press your strength against our chests

lines.original: Oh kudi jatt di fragrance door ton pehchane
lines.translated: That girl recognizes a jatt's fragrance from far away
lines.note: “jatt” — identity term for a tough Punjabi man / bro (see glossary).

lines.original: Asi aashqui ch nave ni shikari aa purane
lines.translated: We're not new hunters in love; we're old ones

lines.original: Gal sukkda kehndi tu jadon supne ch aavein
lines.translated: You say things settle when you come in dreams

lines.original: Naddi ohdon di saundi aa pani rakh ke sirhane
lines.translated: She sleeps like a little stream, putting water by her pillow.
lines.note: Placing a glass/ water by the pillow is a common domestic image; kept literal.

lines.original: Main keha chalde rekaane sikke mitran de khote
lines.translated: I said, as they walk by, friends' coins are counterfeit.
lines.note: 'sikke mitran de khote' literally 'friends' coins are fake' — likely a metaphor for fake friends or false loyalty.

lines.original: Aa ke behnde aa rekaane cheene mitran de kothe
lines.translated: They come and sit, snatching friends' mansions.
lines.note: 'kothe' = large houses/mansions; 'cheene' = seize/steal — literal kept.

lines.original: Tainu sohniye rekaane karaan hathan naal chhavaan
lines.translated: I'll, my beauty, make 'rekaane' — I'll touch you with my hands.
lines.note: 'rekaane' is left untranslated (term unclear from line alone); sentence kept literal and conservative.

lines.original: Jivein karde ne chhavaan sadde pind'an de barote
lines.translated: Like the touches our village's barote give.
lines.note: 'barote' is transliterated — term unclear (local/folk reference); kept literal.

lines.original: Tera sau ton paar para hogyi dhilli kuj khaa ke
lines.translated: You went past a hundred after taking some dhilli.
lines.note: 'dhilli' is ambiguous (could be 'Delhi' the city or Punjabi 'dhilli' = looseness); left literal.

lines.original: Teri 'taar da nazar gun powder chhuva ke
lines.translated: The gaze of your 'taar' brushed against gunpowder.
lines.note: 'taar' is ambiguous (literal 'string/wire' or a figurative element); kept literal as in the line.

lines.original: Eh ton dar na rekaane dekh akhan wich paa ke
lines.translated: Don't be afraid of this — rekaane see it straight in the eyes.
lines.note: Repeated term 'rekaane' left untranslated; note that phrasing is literal to preserve original ambiguity.

lines.original: Jadon chaldi Beretta dindi surma bana ke
lines.translated: When the Beretta walks, it puts on kohl.

lines.original: Kafi kar layi kamai kafi mitran ne udaalayi
lines.translated: I've earned enough money; many friends have spent it.

lines.original: Thode humble aa rekaane Lambo nivi karwali
lines.translated: Some are humble; had the Lambo lowered.

lines.original: Nave shoran ton rekaane gaddi chaldi ni sidhi
lines.translated: Around new guys, the car doesn't drive straight.

lines.original: La daan meech ke ankhan nu back ganne di trolly
lines.translated: With her lips pursed, her eyes like the back of a sugarcane trolley.
lines.note: Literal image is unclear — 'back ganne di trolly' = 'rear of a sugarcane trolley'; likely a local simile for a particular look or posture.

lines.original: Lagge gabru di tod kudi tutti payi nu aave
lines.translated: Seems the girl got broken by the young man's strength.

lines.original: Age naar di fasal jiven phutti payi nu aave
lines.translated: Ahead, a harvest of fire seems to have burst open.
lines.note: 'naar di fasal' is metaphorical — literally 'crop/harvest of fire', implying intense heat, chaos, or fierce scene.

lines.original: Meri yaad di maari nu tainu hichki rekaane
lines.translated: Struck by memories of me, you get hiccups.
lines.note: In some Punjabi usages, 'hichki' (hiccup) is used figuratively to signal sudden remembrance or an involuntary reaction to memory.

lines.original: Sachi barish de gaane wangu sutti payi nu aave
lines.translated: She seems to have fallen asleep like a true rain song

lines.original: Bechain hogyi ikk kudi fan hogyi
lines.translated: One girl got restless, became a fan

lines.original: Aujle de kanna wich nattiyan
lines.translated: Nattiyan in Aujla's ears
lines.note: Aujla refers to (Karan) Aujla; 'nattiyan' are earring-like ornaments.

lines.original: Kari di ni care kude gaul de ni sher kude
lines.translated: The girl doesn't care; the boys speak like lions
lines.note: Line is colloquial and ambiguous. 'kude' here read as 'boys/guys'; 'sher' ('lion') implies boastful or fierce talk.

lines.original: Bhedaan hon baara baari kathiyan
lines.translated: There are differences; they were tied twelve times
lines.note: Literal rendering due to ambiguity. 'Bhedaan' = differences/separations, 'baara baari' = twelve times, 'kathiyan' = tied/stitched.

lines.original: Fan ikko naar da main
lines.translated: I'm a fan of just one woman

lines.original: Das vaari vaarda main
lines.translated: I do it ten times

lines.original: Ohde utton sau sau diyan gatthiyan
lines.translated: Hundreds and hundreds of bundles (knots) on her
lines.note: 'gatthiyan' can mean bundles or knots; left literal.

lines.original: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.translated: It'll take time to get rid of him

lines.original: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.translated: To bring us down

lines.original: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an
lines.translated: Just once, put all your strength in
lines.note: The word "chhatti'an" is ambiguous here; rendered conservatively as 'give it your all.'

lines.original: (Fan ikko naar da main)
lines.translated: (I have a single shout)
lines.note: Original phrasing is ambiguous; literally reads like 'I am of one cry/chant.'

lines.original: (Das vaari vaarda main)
lines.translated: (I hit it ten times)

lines.original: (Ohde utton sau-sau diyan gatthiyan)
lines.translated: (Hundreds of knots/bundles on him)
lines.note: Literal: 'sau-sau diyan gatthiyan' = 'hundreds of knots/bundles.'

lines.original: (Time laggu mitne nu)
lines.translated: (It'll take time to get rid of him)

lines.original: (Sanu thalle sittne nu)
lines.translated: (To bring us down)

lines.original: (Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an)
lines.translated: Just once, give the chests a hard push
lines.note: 'Ikki vaari' = 'just once'; 'jor laa lai' = 'apply force / push hard'. 'chhatti'an' is ambiguous — likely plural of 'chhati' (chest) or a regional slang term; kept a literal reading. Parentheses indicate a backing/aside vocal.
